title: Recession risk
subtitle: Leading and coincident indicators of an economic downturn.

indicators.name: Yield Curve (10yr - 3mo)
indicators.description: The 10-year minus 3-month Treasury constant-maturity spread. A negative reading (inversion) has historically preceded US recessions; the risk window extends through the re-steepening that follows a sustained inversion.
indicators.threshold: Below zero = watch. Within 12 months after a sustained inversion (at least 60 consecutive daily observations below zero) ends = elevated. Otherwise benign.
indicators.notes: The spread is positive (+0.76) and no sustained inversion ended within the trailing 12 months. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Sahm Rule
indicators.description: Rise of the three-month average unemployment rate above its low of the previous twelve months. The published 0.50 trigger has fired at the start of every US recession since 1970 (Sahm 2019).
indicators.threshold: At or above 0.50 = elevated (published Sahm trigger). 0.35-0.50 = watch.
indicators.notes: The Sahm reading of +0.07 is below the watch band. The real-time variant (SAHMREALTIME) is used so historical values reflect data as originally published; construction recomputed from UNRATE. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: High-Yield Credit Spreads
indicators.description: Option-adjusted spread of the ICE BofA US High Yield Index over Treasuries. The cleanest market-priced stress gauge: it widens sharply into downturns, and unusually tight spreads signal complacency rather than safety.
indicators.threshold: At or above 4.00, or widening 100 bp over 63 trading days = watch. At or above 5.50, or widening 200 bp = elevated. At or below 3.00 adds a complacency note without changing status.
indicators.notes: OAS of 2.68 is below the 4.00 watch level with a 63-trading-day change of -16 bp. Complacency note: the spread is at or below 3.00 — historically tight, a sign of reach-for-yield rather than safety. Absolute levels are fixed anchors near the published long-run median and 75th percentile; the keyless FRED endpoint serves this ICE-licensed series from 2023-07-24 only, so window percentiles would drift. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Manufacturing PMI
indicators.description: Purchasing managers' survey of US manufacturing; readings below 50 indicate contraction. The ISM headline is licensed, so the S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI headline is used as the documented free proxy.
indicators.threshold: Below 50 = watch. Below 48 and lower than three months earlier = elevated.
indicators.notes: PMI of 53.9 is above the 50 expansion line. Proxy substitution: ISM headline is licensed; the S&P Global headline is a different survey that can diverge from ISM. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Leading Economic Index
indicators.description: Composite of ten forward-looking inputs built to turn down before the economy does. Status is driven by the six-month growth rate, not the monthly level.
indicators.threshold: Six-month change below zero = watch. At or below -2.0% over six months (about -4% annualised, aligned with the Conference Board's published recession-signal research) = elevated.
indicators.notes: The six-month change of -0.3% is negative. Monthly change +0.1%. Licence note: only the publicly announced headline figures are displayed; no licensed series is redistributed. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Labour Market
indicators.description: Unemployment rate and payroll momentum, checked against internals (long-term unemployed, continued claims). These confirm a downturn once underway; the trend matters more than the headline level.
indicators.threshold: Unemployment 3-month average at least 0.20 pp above its 12-month low, payroll 3-month average below 75k, or softening internals (long-term unemployed over 15% YoY and continued claims over 5% in 26 weeks) = watch. Rise at or above 0.35 pp or negative payroll 3-month average = elevated.
indicators.notes: Headline and internals are steady: unemployment rise +0.07 pp, payroll 3-month average +111k, long-term unemployed +17% YoY, continued claims -3.7% over 26 weeks. Inputs: UNRATE, PAYEMS, UEMP27OV (BLS), CCSA (DOL), all via FRED and cross-checked at the originating agencies. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Valuation
indicators.description: Price divided by the ten-year average of inflation-adjusted earnings. A high reading means an expensive market and weak long-run return expectations — it does not time tops, and markets can stay expensive for years.
indicators.threshold: Context only — never a timing trigger. Flagged when above 30 as a long-run return caveat.
indicators.notes: Method note: the headline is multpl's daily CAPE on the latest price with lagged earnings; the chart is the monthly Shiller series (public Yale dataset), so the last monthly point sits slightly below the daily figure in a rising market. Cross-checked within tolerance at first use; see VERIFICATION.md. The reading is far above long-run norms (multpl long-run mean 17.39, median 16.10), which historically maps to weak decade-ahead returns — a caveat on future returns, not a recession timer.
